title: Resinous Australian Coconut Hash
description: A crispy, energizing breakfast dish featuring shredded coconut and a hint of flambe, suitable for those following a macrobiotic diet.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the shredded coconut, macadamia nuts, and dried apricots.
In a separate saucepan, heat the coconut oil over low heat.
Add the honey, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt to the saucepan and stir until well combined.
Pour the saucepan mixture over the coconut mixture and toss until the coconut is evenly coated.
Transfer the coconut mixture to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
While the coconut is baking, heat a small amount of ghee in a skillet over medium heat.
When the coconut is done, add it to the skillet with the ghee and stir until the coconut is crispy and golden brown.
Add a pinch of cayenne pepper and a sprinkle of fresh thyme to the skillet and stir to combine.
Squeeze a sliver of lime juice over the coconut and stir to combine.
Drizzle the coconut with a small amount of maple syrup and stir to combine.
Stir in the chia seeds, coconut cream, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, and star anise until well combined.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the Resinous Australian Coconut Hash warm, garnished with additional fresh thyme and a sprinkle of cayenne pepper if desired.
Garnish with toasted walnuts if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
